artifacts.summary: Official discovery found one substantive University of Calcutta policy source: the 2025 Intellectual Property Policy and its IPR summary page. Claims are limited to the policy's generative-AI language.

targets.sourceTitle: Intellectual Property (IP) Policy of University of Calcutta Version 1.0

stopConditions: Stop after official policy PDF yields citation-ready evidence for AI-related claims.
Do not use curriculum-only pages for policy claims.
Do not bypass the Sucuri JavaScript challenge on the root HTML site.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Intellectual Property (IP) Policy of University of Calcutta Version 1.0

artifacts.claimText: The University of Calcutta IP Policy says the university is not responsible for copyright/IP violations when generative AI is used to generate content for publication, thesis, dissertation, or course material, and says faculty members, students, researchers, and staff will be held accountable for AI authorship associated with LLMs.
artifacts.normalizedValue: AI-generated content for publication, thesis, dissertation, or course material is linked to user accountability for AI authorship under the IP Policy.

citation.citationTitle: Intellectual Property (IP) Policy of University of Calcutta Version 1.0
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Calcutta IP Policy says generative AI may be used responsibly and ethically, if at all, for fine-tuning, analyzing, or summarizing content, and says that use needs to be disclosed with the model name, version, source, description, and usage.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Responsible and ethical generative AI use for fine-tuning, analyzing, or summarizing content needs disclosure of model details and usage.

citation.citationTitle: Intellectual Property (IP) Policy of University of Calcutta Version 1.0
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Calcutta IP Policy strongly discourages the university fraternity from uploading sensitive and confidential documents into generative AI tools to prevent leakage of proprietary information.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Strongly discourages uploading sensitive and confidential documents to generative AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: Intellectual Property (IP) Policy of University of Calcutta Version 1.0
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: The University of Calcutta IP Policy says generative AI tools may be allowed for checking spelling and grammar, improving readability, checking plagiarism, and conducting research on AI itself.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Generative AI tools may be allowed for spelling and grammar checks, readability improvement, plagiarism checking, and AI research.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Intellectual Property (IP) Policy of University of Calcutta Version 1.0
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University will not be responsible for copyright/IP violations in instances where generative AI is used to generate content for publication, thesis, dissertation, or course material. The faculty members/students/researchers/staff will be held accountable for the AI authorship associated with Large Language Models (LLMs).

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Generative AI tools may be allowed on the following grounds a. checking spelling and Grammar; b. improving readability; c. checking plagiarism; d. conducting research on AI itself.

artifacts.title: University of Calcutta AI Policy Evidence Draft
artifacts.summary: Agent-reviewed staged draft based on the University of Calcutta 2025 IP Policy. The policy includes specific generative-AI language on IP accountability, disclosure, discouraged use of AI-generated scholarly content, sensitive/confidential documents, and limited allowed grounds.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
This staged run cites the university IP Policy only; curriculum-only AI pages were rejected as insufficient for policy claims.
Claims remain staged agent-reviewed candidates and are not canonical public claims.
